name: Butter Paneer Masala with Roti
description: Velvety tomato-cashew gravy with paneer cubes, rich with butter and cream — the ultimate comfort curry.
steps: Simmer tomatoes, onion, cashews, and ginger-garlic paste with a cup of water for 15 minutes. Cool and blend smooth.
Melt butter in a pan, add the blended tomato-cashew puree. Cook on medium heat, stirring, until oil separates.
Add red chilli, coriander powder, and salt. Cook 5 minutes.
Add paneer cubes and a splash of water to loosen. Simmer 5 minutes.
Finish with cream, garam masala, and crushed kasuri methi. Serve with hot rotis.

name: Kadhai Paneer with Roti
description: Paneer and peppers tossed in a smoky, coarsely ground kadhai masala with tomatoes and onion — bold and rustic.
steps: Heat oil in a kadhai (wok), add crushed coriander seeds and dry red chilli. Sauté 30 seconds.
Add ginger-garlic paste, then onion. Cook until golden.
Add tomatoes and cook until completely broken down and oil separates.
Add capsicum and paneer. Toss on high flame for 3–4 minutes to get some char.
Finish with garam masala and kasuri methi. Serve straight from the kadhai with roti.

name: Soya Masala with Roti
description: Soya chunks simmered in a spiced onion-tomato masala — hearty, protein-rich, and deeply flavoured.
steps: Boil soya chunks in salted water for 5 minutes. Squeeze out water thoroughly.
Heat oil, add onion and cook until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes.
Add tomato puree with all dry spices. Cook until oil separates.
Add soya chunks and 0.5 cup water. Simmer 10 minutes until masala coats well.
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.

name: Rajma Chawal
description: Dark kidney beans slow-cooked in a thick, spiced onion-tomato gravy, served over steamed jeera rice.
steps: Pressure cook soaked rajma with salt for 5–6 whistles until completely tender.
Heat oil, sauté onion until deep golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, cook 2 minutes.
Add tomato puree and all spices. Cook until oil separates.
Add cooked rajma with its liquid. Mash a few beans for thickness. Simmer 15 minutes.
Cook basmati rice separately. Serve rajma over rice with a dollop of ghee and raw onion rings.

name: Kadi Chawal
description: Tangy yoghurt and besan kadi with soft pakoras, tempered with ghee and dried red chillies, over steamed rice.
steps: Whisk yoghurt and besan with water, turmeric, chilli, and salt until smooth and lump-free.
Bring to a boil in a heavy pan, stirring continuously to prevent lumps. Simmer 25 minutes on low.
Make small besan pakoras (optional) and drop into the simmering kadi.
For tadka: heat ghee, splutter mustard seeds, fenugreek, dry red chilli, and curry leaves. Pour over kadi.
Serve over steamed basmati rice with a side of papad.

name: Dal Chawal
description: Simple pressure-cooked toor dal tempered with ghee, cumin, garlic, and dried red chilli over fluffy rice.
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with tomato, onion, green chilli, turmeric, and salt for 4 whistles. Mash well.
Adjust consistency with water and simmer 5 minutes.
For tadka: heat ghee until smoking, add cumin seeds and dry red chilli. Add sliced garlic and cook until golden.
Pour tadka over dal. Cover for 2 minutes to let the flavour infuse.
Serve over steamed rice with a squeeze of lemon and papad.

name: Sev Tamatar with Roti
description: A quick, tangy Gujarati tomato curry thickened with sev — one of those humble dishes that punches far above its weight.
steps: Heat oil, splutter mustard seeds. Add green chilli, ginger-garlic paste, and onion. Sauté until golden.
Add chopped tomatoes with turmeric, red chilli, sugar, and salt. Cook until completely soft and saucy.
Add 0.5 cup water and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add sev and stir gently. Cook 2 minutes — sev will thicken the curry.
Garnish with coriander and serve immediately with roti before sev goes too soft.

name: Chole with Roti
description: Dark, smoky chickpeas cooked with tea bag for colour and a bold spice mix — the real Amritsari chole.
steps: Pressure cook soaked chana with the tea bag and salt for 6–7 whistles. Discard tea bag.
Heat oil, cook onion until deep brown. Add ginger-garlic paste, cook 2 minutes.
Add tomato puree, chole masala, and amchur. Cook until oil separates.
Add cooked chana with some of its liquid. Mash a few for thickness. Simmer 15 minutes.
Finish with garam masala. Garnish with coriander and ginger juliennes. Serve with bhatura or roti.

name: Sprouts ki Sabji with Roti
description: Mixed sprouted lentils stir-fried with tomato, onion and simple spices — nutritious, light, and quick.
steps: Heat oil, splutter cumin seeds. Add ginger, green chilli, and onion. Sauté until soft.
Add tomato with turmeric and coriander powder. Cook until tomatoes break down.
Add sprouts and 0.25 cup water. Cover and cook 8–10 minutes on medium heat.
Check that sprouts are tender but not mushy.
Finish with lemon juice and coriander. Serve warm with roti.

name: Paneer Ghee Roast with Roti
description: Paneer tossed in a fiery, tangy Mangalorean dry-roasted spice paste with ghee — bold, aromatic, and unforgettable.
steps: Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin, and peppercorns until fragrant. Cool.
Blend roasted spices with red chillies, garlic, tamarind, and a little water into a smooth paste.
Heat ghee, fry curry leaves until crisp. Add the spice paste and cook on medium heat until ghee separates.
Add jaggery and salt. Toss in paneer cubes and coat well.
Cook on high heat 3–4 minutes until paneer is slightly charred. Serve with neer dosa or roti.

name: Bhindi with Roti
description: Crispy stir-fried okra with onion, tomato, and spices — the key is high heat and no lid to keep it from getting slimy.
steps: Wipe bhindi completely dry — moisture causes sliminess.
Heat oil on high, add cumin seeds. Add bhindi and spread in a single layer. Do not stir for 2 minutes.
Toss and cook on high heat, stirring occasionally, for 8–10 minutes until crispy.
Add onion, green chilli, tomato, and all spices. Cook 5 more minutes.
Finish with amchur. Serve with hot roti.

name: Cluster Beans with Roti
description: Gavar phali (cluster beans) cooked with peanuts in a lightly spiced dry Gujarati style — nutty and wholesome.
steps: Blanch cluster beans in salted boiling water for 3 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Heat oil, splutter mustard and sesame seeds. Add ginger-chilli paste and onion, sauté until soft.
Add cluster beans with turmeric and salt. Stir-fry on medium heat 5–6 minutes.
Add peanuts and sugar. Toss together.
Finish with lemon juice and serve with roti.

name: Methi Malai Paneer with Roti
description: Creamy white gravy with fresh fenugreek leaves and paneer — mildly spiced, slightly bitter, and luxuriously rich.
steps: Blanch methi in salted water 2 minutes to reduce bitterness. Squeeze dry and chop.
Heat oil, cook onion paste until light golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli.
Add cashew paste and cook 3 minutes. Add methi and cook 4 minutes.
Add cream, sugar, salt, and garam masala. Stir and simmer 5 minutes.
Add paneer cubes. Cook gently 5 minutes. Serve with roti.

name: Baingan Bharta with Roti
description: Flame-roasted aubergine mashed and cooked with onion, tomato, and spices — smoky, earthy, and deeply comforting.
steps: Rub baingan with oil and roast directly on a gas flame, turning, until completely charred and collapsed. Cool.
Peel off charred skin. Mash the flesh roughly with a fork.
Heat oil, cook garlic and ginger until golden. Add onion and cook until brown.
Add tomatoes, green chilli, red chilli, and salt. Cook until oil separates.
Add mashed baingan and garam masala. Cook 5 minutes. Garnish with coriander and serve with roti.

name: Paneer Bhurji with Roti
description: Scrambled paneer with onion, tomato, capsicum and spices — quick, protein-rich, and satisfying.
steps: Heat oil, splutter cumin seeds. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli, cook 1 minute.
Add onion and sauté until golden. Add capsicum and cook 2 minutes.
Add tomato with turmeric, red chilli, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down.
Add crumbled paneer and toss well on high heat for 3 minutes.
Finish with kasuri methi and coriander. Serve with roti.

name: Jeera Aloo with Roti
description: Boiled potatoes tossed in a cumin-forward dry spice mix with coriander and amchur — simple, honest, perfect.
steps: Heat ghee until shimmering. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle and darken slightly.
Add green chilli and ginger. Sauté 30 seconds.
Add boiled potato cubes and spread in the pan. Let them sit 2 minutes to get a crust.
Add turmeric, coriander powder, amchur, red chilli, and salt. Toss to coat.
Cook on medium-high heat, tossing occasionally, until potatoes are slightly crispy. Garnish with coriander.

name: Veg Pulao with Raita
description: Fragrant basmati rice cooked with whole spices and seasonal vegetables, served with cooling boondi raita.
steps: Heat ghee, add bay leaf and whole spices. Add sliced onion, cook until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and vegetables. Sauté 3 minutes.
Add drained rice and toss gently to coat with ghee and spices.
Add water and salt. Bring to boil, then cover and cook on low heat 15 minutes. Rest 5 minutes.
For raita: whisk yoghurt with salt and cumin. Stir in boondi just before serving.

name: Dum Aloo with Roti
description: Baby potatoes slow-cooked on dum in a yoghurt-based Kashmiri red gravy — aromatic, rich, and deeply satisfying.
steps: Prick baby potatoes all over with a fork.
Heat mustard oil until smoking, reduce heat. Fry potatoes until golden on all sides. Remove.
In the same oil, add cardamom, cloves, and asafoetida. Add whisked yoghurt and stir vigorously.
Add kashmiri chilli, fennel, ginger powder, and salt. Cook until oil separates.
Return potatoes to the pan. Cover tightly and cook on low heat (dum) for 15 minutes. Serve with roti.

name: Kala Chana with Roti
description: Black chickpeas slow-cooked in an earthy, robust masala — nutty, hearty, and deeply nutritious.
steps: Pressure cook soaked kala chana with salt for 7–8 whistles until tender.
Heat oil, cook onion until deep brown. Add ginger-garlic paste, cook 2 minutes.
Add tomato puree with all dry spices. Cook until oil separates.
Add cooked chana with a cup of its liquid. Simmer 15 minutes.
Finish with garam masala, amchur, and coriander. Serve with roti.

name: Malai Kofta with Roti
description: Soft paneer and potato dumplings in a velvety cashew-onion cream sauce — restaurant-style indulgence at home.
steps: Mix grated paneer, mashed potato, cornflour, and salt. Shape into smooth balls. Fry until golden. Set aside.
Cook onion paste in oil until light golden. Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates.
Add cashew paste, garam masala, cardamom, and salt. Cook 5 minutes.
Add cream and a splash of water to get a thick, pourable gravy. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add koftas just before serving — do not overcook or they will break. Serve immediately with roti.

name: Dal Tadka with Jeera Rice
description: Dhaba-style yellow dal with a fiery ghee tadka of garlic, red chilli, and cumin over jeera rice.
steps: Pressure cook mixed dals with tomato, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and salt for 4 whistles. Mash.
Sauté onion in 1 tbsp ghee until golden, add to dal. Simmer 10 minutes.
Cook basmati rice with 1 tsp ghee and 1 tsp cumin seeds in water until fluffy.
For tadka: heat remaining ghee until smoking. Add cumin, dry red chilli, and garlic. Fry until golden.
Pour sizzling tadka directly over dal. Serve with jeera rice and lemon wedge.

name: Dal Makhani with Roti
description: Slow-cooked black urad dal with kidney beans, simmered overnight in butter and cream — the ultimate Punjabi indulgence.
steps: Pressure cook urad dal and rajma with salt for 8–10 whistles until very soft.
Heat butter, cook onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato puree. Cook until oil separates.
Add cooked dal and rajma. Mash some dal against the side of the pot for creaminess.
Simmer on very low heat for 30–40 minutes, stirring often. Add water as needed.
Finish with cream, garam masala, and kasuri methi. A longer slow cook = better flavour.

name: Vegetable Khichdi with Curd
description: One-pot rice and moong dal khichdi loaded with vegetables, tempered with ghee and cumin — the original comfort food.
steps: Wash and soak rice and dal together for 15 minutes.
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, splutter cumin seeds. Add ginger and onion, sauté until soft.
Add tomato and turmeric. Cook 2 minutes.
Add vegetables, drained rice-dal, water, and salt. Pressure cook 3 whistles.
Rest 5 minutes. Open, stir and adjust consistency. Serve with curd, pickle, and papad.

name: Matar Paneer with Roti
description: Green peas and paneer in a spiced tomato-onion gravy — a staple weekday curry that never disappoints.
steps: Heat oil, cook onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, cook 2 minutes.
Add tomato puree with all dry spices. Cook until oil separates.
Add green peas and 0.5 cup water. Cover and cook 5 minutes.
Add paneer cubes and simmer 5 minutes.
Finish with garam masala and coriander. Serve with roti.

name: Besan Gatta with Roti
description: Spiced chickpea flour dumplings simmered in a tangy yoghurt gravy — a Rajasthani classic with bold, earthy character.
steps: Mix besan with ajwain, half the spices, 1 tbsp oil, and salt. Add water gradually to form a stiff dough.
Roll into cylinders (gattas). Boil in salted water 10 minutes until firm. Slice into pieces.
Whisk yoghurt with remaining spices, besan, and water into a smooth slurry.
Heat oil, temper mustard seeds, cumin, and dry red chilli. Add yoghurt mixture and bring to boil, stirring.
Add gatta pieces and simmer 10 minutes until gravy thickens. Serve with roti.

name: Thepla with Curd
description: Soft, spiced whole wheat and methi flatbreads that stay fresh for days — a Gujarati travel staple and everyday comfort.
steps: Combine flour, besan, methi, green chilli-ginger paste, yoghurt, spices, sesame, ajwain, and salt.
Add 1 tbsp oil and knead into a smooth, pliable dough. Add water only if needed — yoghurt usually suffices.
Rest dough 10 minutes. Divide and roll into thin rounds.
Cook on a hot tawa with a little oil on both sides until golden spots appear.
Serve warm or at room temperature with cold curd and mango pickle.
